context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop on Left

episode_text:
day=3; meal=general tso's chicken and broccoli; setting=Working on laptop with laptop on Left; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is cold-served fruit or dessert AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is personal or social THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed cold-food meal used no reheating, consistent with intended cold serving. IF food is a warm savory meal AND affective state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop THEN 3 min BECAUSE two observed laptop-context savory meals were reheated for 3 min, indicating a stable preference for thoroughly warming hot foods in that multitasking context.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a salient person or focal activity in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user selected front-occlusion minimization in both a front-social context and repeated laptop-working contexts, showing stable concern about preserving the forward view when attention is directed forward. No left- or right-specific variation observed yet.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social or involves a concurrent visually attended activity AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected slow speed across all observed meals, suggesting a stable comfort-first interaction style rather than prioritizing speed. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected this across different foods and contexts, favoring reliable bite acquisition. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social or involves a concurrent task AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food requiring bite acquisition THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected explicit web confirmation in all observed meals, indicating a stable preference to verify bite acquisition before transfer. This fits the user's ability to access the web interface with assistive input and suggests they accept extra interaction time to reduce transfer errors.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to limited trunk control, and all observed meals used inside-mouth transfer. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE outside-mouth distance does not apply, and the user has consistently selected inside-mouth transfer. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected a quiet nonverbal cue in the observed social meal. IF dining context involves working on a laptop AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task THEN LED or speech + LED, with repeated evidence favoring LED BECAUSE the user has selected multimodal signaling once and LED in a later repeated laptop context, indicating a general preference for nonverbal signaling with some tolerance for stronger cues when multitasking.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is feeding THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and previously selected open-mouth readiness in a social meal, indicating low-effort initiation can outweigh social ambiguity concerns. IF dining context involves working on a laptop AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is feeding THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected autocontinue in repeated laptop contexts, suggesting that when attention is divided, minimizing interaction burden takes priority over explicit initiation.

IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is drinking THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and previously selected open-mouth readiness for drink initiation. IF dining context involves working on a laptop AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected autocontinue in repeated laptop contexts, again showing preference for lower-effort automation when attention is divided.

IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is mouth wiping THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and previously selected open-mouth readiness for wiping initiation. IF dining context involves working on a laptop AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected autocontinue in repeated laptop contexts, consistent with preferring reduced interaction demand when occupied.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected nonverbal completion cues in the observed social meal. IF dining context involves working on a laptop AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task THEN LED or speech + LED, with repeated evidence favoring LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal completion cues once but later selected LED again in a repeated laptop context, suggesting a generally quiet cueing style even while multitasking.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF task is feeding AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected sensor-based bite completion detection in all observed meals, and this avoids button presses the user cannot reliably perform. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF task is drinking AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or perform head gestures, and all observed meals used autocontinue. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF task is mouth wiping AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or perform head gestures, and all observed meals used autocontinue. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a salient person or focal activity in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting between bites in both a front-social context and repeated laptop-working contexts, indicating willingness to accept longer meal time to reduce prolonged visual obstruction when the forward view matters. No evidence yet that the user prefers retracting in quiet personal settings without a competing visual focus.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food has no dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the observed social meal with whipped cream used less dipping, consistent with limiting messiness. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed laptop meal with ranch dressing used no dipping, suggesting a stronger mess-avoidance preference when multitasking around equipment.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a moderate wait in the observed social meal, balancing time to respond with meal efficiency. IF dining context involves working on a laptop AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected the longest wait in repeated laptop contexts, indicating a strong preference for extra time before automatic progression when attention is divided.
